subject: Statistics
title: Wayne Gretzky's Unbreakable Records
description: A short lesson to help you apply Wayne Gretzky's Unbreakable Records.

hook.text: Wayne Gretzky has 2,857 career points. The second-place player has 1,921. The gap is nearly 1,000 points.

buildup.text: Gretzky is such a statistical outlier that he breaks most hockey analyses. He's a different data universe.

discovery.text: If you remove all of Gretzky's goals, he still leads the all-time scoring list on assists alone.

twist.text: Hockey scoring has dropped dramatically since Gretzky's era. The average goals per game went from 8 to 6.

climax.text: Even adjusted for era, Gretzky's stats are the most dominant in any major sport. The gap is real.

punchline.text: Remove his goals. He's still the all-time leader. That's dominance.

quiz.question: What makes Wayne Gretzky's career stats so remarkable?
options: Even without any goals, his assists alone would make him the all-time leading scorer
He played more games than anyone else
He only played against weak teams
